title: agasyllis caucasica
intro: Agasyllis caucasica, also known as Caucasian bugloss or Alangium, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, belonging to the family Cornaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that help in treating wounds and skin infections. Agasyllis caucasica is known to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
